questions.question: What is the primary purpose of the 'Demo Dojo'?

options.text: To practice your demo flow in a low-stakes environment against an AI

questions.explanation: You don't want to practice on real leads. That burns cash. The Dojo allows you to get the 'reps' in without risking real opportunities.

questions.question: When roleplaying, what should you focus on first?
questions.explanation: Before you get fancy with humor or objection handling, you must master the core mechanics of the demo structure. Nail the blocks first.

questions.question: How do you know if you are talking too much?

options.text: Review your 'Talk-to-Listen' ratio afterwards (or notice if you haven't asked a question in 5 mins)

questions.explanation: A healthy demo is a conversation, not a monologue. Tools like Gong (or our AI feedback) will flag if your monologue streaks are top long.

questions.question: What is 'Game Tape'?

options.text: Recording your practice sessions (and real calls) to review them objectively

questions.explanation: Just like athletes watch game tape to fix their form, founders must watch their own demos to spot 'umms', mouse wiggling, and missed buying signals.

questions.question: If the AI gives you a tough objection, what should you do?
questions.explanation: The point of the Dojo is to simulate the 'Hard Mode'. Use the frameworks you learned (Clarify, Isolate, Reframe) to navigate the objection.
